str:translations.str:about_feeds: This is a web feed, also known as an Atom feed. Subscribe by copying the URL from the address bar into your newsreader. Visit About Feeds to learn more and get started. It's free.

feed.title: ~/nbaig-dev - documentation
feed.subtitle: I am a backend web developer with 14+ years of experience. In this blog I will be posting my journey with the Rust Programming Language.

entry.title: The Importance of Understanding Agile and Scrum Principles
entry.summary: In this blog post, we delve into the importance of understanding Agile and Scrum principles in modern software development projects. With an emphasis on collaboration, iterative development, customer feedback, self-organizing teams, continuous improvement, flexibility, timeboxing, and documentation over excessive documentation, these methodologies help teams deliver better software faster while remaining adaptable to changing requirements. From the role of teamwork, collaboration, and communication in Agile and Scrum projects to the significance of delivering value, customer feedback, self-organizing teams, continuous improvement, flexibility, timeboxing, and documentation over excessive documentation, this post highlights the key principles and practices associated with these methodologies. As software development continues to evolve at a rapid pace, understanding Agile and Scrum principles is crucial for success in today's projects. By embracing these principles, teams can deliver high-quality software faster while remaining flexible to changing requirements, ultimately leading to better outcomes for customers, stakeholders, and end-users alike. So, whether you're a seasoned software development professional or just starting out, we encourage you to take the time to learn more about Agile and Scrum principles and how they can benefit your team. Share this post with your colleagues, friends, and social networks, and let's continue to push the boundaries of what's possible in software development together!

entry.title: How to Use Documentation as a Learning Tool
entry.summary: In today's fast-paced world of software development, documentation is often overlooked as a mere requirement to fulfill rather than a valuable resource that can help developers learn and grow. However, this blog post argues that documentation can serve as a powerful learning tool for new or junior developers in several ways. In this article, we present ten points that illustrate the various benefits of using documentation as a learning resource. The post begins by highlighting the importance of understanding the system's architecture and design decisions, followed by detailed instructions on how to use each component. Next, it emphasizes the need for explaining error messages and providing insights into system testing, security, performance, and roadmap. Finally, it suggests guidelines for contributing to the project and encourages readers to share the post with their peers and colleagues. This blog post is written in a clear and concise style, using plain language and avoiding technical jargon wherever possible. It is accessible to developers of all levels and serves as an excellent resource for anyone looking to improve their documentation skills or learn about a new system.

entry.title: How to Break Out of the Tutorial Rut and Start Building Your Own Projects
entry.summary: In today's fast-paced tech industry, it's easy to get stuck in the tutorial rut - constantly consuming content but never applying what you've learned. However, building your own projects is the key to breaking free from this cycle and gaining valuable practical experience. In this blog post, we share ten actionable points that can help you transition from tutorials to real-world project development. From defining your project idea and identifying learning objectives to collaborating with others, testing, launching, and continuously improving - this guide covers everything you need to know to build a successful product. So whether you're a beginner or an experienced developer, this post is a must-read for anyone looking to take their skills to the next level!

entry.title: How to Deal with the Pressure of Being a Full Stack Developer
entry.summary: In today's fast-paced tech industry, full stack development has become an essential part of creating cutting-edge web applications. However, with such significant responsibility comes immense pressure that can take a toll on a developer's mental and physical wellbeing. In this blog post, we explore ten practical strategies to help full stack developers manage the pressure associated with their work effectively. From prioritizing tasks, communicating effectively, and embracing continuous learning, to managing time wisely, staying calm under pressure, testing early and often, staying organized and documented, and staying passionate about your work, these tips cover everything from time management and communication skills to staying up-to-date with the latest technologies. By implementing these strategies in their daily workflow, full stack developers can stay organized, focused, and productive over time, and continue contributing to the exciting world of full stack development. Let's take a closer look at each tip and explore how it can help us better manage pressure as a full stack developer!
